data.subject: [Product Update] Google Cloud: Update to default resource-based CUD sharing setting

body.content:
We’ll enable resource-based CUD sharing by default to maximize savings.
MY CONSOLE
 

Hello Adam,

Starting June 16, 2026, the default configuration for resource-based Commitment Use Discount (CUD) sharing will change from disabled to enabled. This update is designed to maximize savings and reduce underused CUDs across your resources.

What you need to know

Key changes:

  • New billing accounts: All new billing accounts created on or after June 16, 2026 will have CUD sharing enabled by default.
  • Existing billing accounts (no active resource-based CUDs): Accounts that do not have active resource-based CUDs as of June 16, 2026, will have their default CUD sharing setting updated to enabled.
  • Existing billing accounts (with active resource-based CUDs): If you have at least one active resource-based CUD on your billing account at the time of the change, your current settings will remain unchanged.
  • Flexible management: You can enable or disable CUD sharing for your billing account at any time using the self-serve toggle in the Google Cloud Billing console.

Potential impact:
This change is designed to be value-additive. With sharing enabled, any resource-based commitments you purchase can be applied to eligible resources in any project linked to your billing account, rather than being restricted to a single project. This helps prevent "stranded" discounts and maximizes your total savings.

What you need to do

Action advised:
No immediate action is required to receive this benefit.

However, if you prefer to keep your discounts isolated to a specific project, you may use the new self-serve toggle in the Google Cloud Billing console to disable sharing at any time.

Timelines:
The default update will take effect on June 16, 2026. You will have access to the self-serve management tools in the Google Cloud Billing console prior to this date to adjust your preferences.

Impacted customers/accounts:
This change impacts new billing accounts and existing accounts without active resource-based CUDs.

Please refer to the attachment below (CSV file) for a list of the projects.

We’re here to help

For more information on how resource-based CUD sharing works and how to manage it, please refer to our Share resource-based CUDs across projects documentation.

If you have any questions or require assistance, please contact Google Cloud Support.

Thanks for choosing Google Cloud.

– The Google Cloud Team
DOCUMENTATION
SUPPORT

Was this information helpful?

       

© 2026 Google LLC 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, CA 94043

You’ve received this mandatory service announcement to update you about important changes to Google Cloud or your account.

data.subject: Project Management: Skills and Techniques

body.content:
You don't often get email from lili.shepherd@skillboosters.org. Learn why this is important

Hello Adam,

Hope you are doing well.

We would be glad to invite you for our one day Project Management Techniques workshop planned in your city.

Project Management is all about being efficient and effective and in this workshop you will learn the concepts, techniques and processes that will help you lead projects successfully and motivate your group. In the course we find how project management enables organizational outcomes in terms of project selection, initiation, planning, implementation, monitoring and controlling.

The key constraints of Scope, Time, and Cost are integrated with Earned Value Management (EVM), to monitor and accurately estimate project outcomes.

Cities: All Major Cities in the USA & Canada

Dates: | March 27, 2026 | April 24, 2026 | May 29, 2026 |

What you will learn:

  • Determine goals, objectives, and requirements of a project
  • Understand planning: what it does and does not do
  • Create a precedence network diagram to determine early and late schedules
  • Analyze the critical path and determine duration compression options
  • How to use Gantt and Milestone charts
  • Implement and maintain performance measurement baselines
  • Establish guidelines for successful closeout
  • Apply general management skills to projects

Key Features:

  • 8 hours intensive training on Project Management techniques
  • Learn the managerial concepts and techniques that will help you deliver results
  • Be a more effective and efficient manager
  • Learn through hands on practical exercises
  • Get course completion certificate

Our other courses: PMP | Conflict Management | ITIL | PMI ACP | Agile and Scrum | SALESFORCE | LSSGB | LSSBB

Please mention your city name while replying to this email so that we can provide your city specific information. Look forward to your participation.

For more details, please reach the training managers on 832-548-0612

Warm Regards,

Lili Shepherd
Marketing Executive

body.content:
You don't often get email from channels@suse.com. Learn why this is important

 
 
 
 
 
Newsletter
 
 
 
SUSE One
Partner Pulse
 
Partner Portal
 
Dear  Adam

We hope you're having a great start to the week! In this edition of our partner newsletter, we're excited to share the latest featured news, updates, and resources specifically for you.

View Your News Archive   
         
Partner Portal   
         
SUSE Partner Loyalty Platform
 
 

Featured News

 
 
 

Plan Your SUSECON 2026 Experience

 

Get ready to head to Prague from April 20–23 for SUSECON 2026, where the theme "Shape Your Resilient Future" takes center stage. With over 100 technical sessions and 120-minute hands-on labs available, you can now curate a personalized track focused on the innovations most relevant to your business by accessing the newly launched session scheduler. Log in today to reserve your seat in high-demand technical deep dives and ensure you don’t miss the opportunity to earn complimentary SUSE certifications on-site. If you haven’t registered to attend yet and want to save on the full access pass, reach out to your SUSE contact for a 20% discount code.

 
Schedule Your Sessions
 
 

All Things Product

 
 
 

March 2026 Product Announcements

 

New Product Release


  • 20676 - Simplified Multi-Linux Support part numbers and transparent pricing for FY26 MLA/VLA Details
  • 20677 - Simplified Multi-Linux Support part numbers and transparent pricing for FY26 IHV/ISV Details
  • 20678 - Simplified Multi-Linux Support part numbers and transparent pricing for FY26 MSP Details

Search Product Announce­ments
 
 
 

Modernize Without Compromise: Future-Proof Your Virtualization

 

Modernizing your virtualization stack doesn't have to mean compromising on performance or open-source flexibility. Explore the critical technical considerations for implementing a robust strategy that prioritizes choice and eliminates vendor lock-in. Use these insights to optimize your infrastructure and drive seamless innovation across hybrid cloud environments.

 
Explore Virtualization Tips
 
 

Selling SUSE

 
 
 

The Trusted Foundation for SAP Business One on HANA

 

As the only operating system certified for SAP Business One (B1) since its inception, SUSE provides the most reliable on-premises foundation for customers seeking local control and performance. Whether migrating from SQL to HANA or scaling up to 600+ users, your clients benefit from dedicated B1 installation modules and high-availability components that eliminate unplanned downtime. Position the world’s leading platform for B1 to ensure your customers' mission-critical ERP environments remain stable, secure, and fully supported.

 
Maximize B1 Performance
 
 

Industry & Ecosystem

 
 
 

Streamline Innovation with SLES 16

 

SUSE's ISV Partners need a Linux foundation that allows them to focus on innovation, not operating system complexity and long-term support overhead. Join us for an in-depth session on SUSE Linux Enterprise Server 16 on Tuesday, March 31, 2026 11:00am ET and discover how a modern Linux foundation can reduce risk, control cost, and accelerate competitive innovation across your product lifecycle.

 
Register Now
 

Until next time!

Best regards

HI Marc,

 

Please find below the answers to your questions.

 

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.

 

From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  

 

We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 

 

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.

I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

 

Kindly revert on any clarifications.

 

Thanks & Regards

 

Rupesh Sankar

Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace

 

Good morning TDSynnex,

Kindly submit responses by EOD, we are looking to make a final decision.

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852

From: Matthew Carter
Sent: March 9, 2026 8:15 PM
To: 'Thomson, Christine' <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>; Tammo, Gina <gina.tammo@tdsynnex.com>; Sankar, Rupesh <rupesh.sankar@tdsynnex.com>
Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace

 

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh

Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:

 

  • Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
    • Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
    • StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
    • In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
    • If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
    • Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?
  • Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
    • Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
    • The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
    • We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
  • API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
    • Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
  • What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
    • Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
    • Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
      Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
      Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
      • On average, how much MDF credits can we anticipate receiving monthly/annually from the TDSynnex program? We would like to see details as this is important to us.
  • Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
    • Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources. Again, who would be part of our team, Gina?
    • Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
    • Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
  • Net 90 terms
    • Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility If 90 is denied for whatever reason, what is your standard, 60? 2% Net 10?
      • If Aptum has an AWS customer that goes bankrupt or denies us payment, what programs do you have to reduce this burden? Historically, we’ve had to pay for AWS consumption and cannot collect from a bankrupt customer…
      • Besides 10th of the following month billing; what other sweeteners can you add to incentivize Aptum to award TDSynnex over a competitor?

 

 

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: March 3, 2026 5:07 PM
To: Thomson, Christine <Christine.Thomson@tdsynnex.com>
Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace

AWS Distribution Partnership Discussion

Date: Recent
Attendees: Aptum team (@Matthew Carter, @Marc Pare) & TDS team (@Sankar, @Rupesh, @Campbell, @Michael, @Tammo, @Gina)

 

Executive Summary

Aptum is evaluating moving their AWS distribution partnership to TDS to address customer visibility concerns and improve margin structure. Key opportunity: $2M from a $5M AWS customer account, targeting 6% margins with 3% customer pass-through.

 

Key Discussion Points

Current Situation

  • Customer can see aggregator discounts via IAM access → causing dissatisfaction
  • Current margin: 4% from existing distributor
  • Customer considering going direct to AWS without better pricing
  • Decision needed: Move DSA to TDS before April 1st deadline

 

April 1st AWS Program Changes

Major structural changes:

  • Partners receive full 8% front-end discount (up from 3%)
  • Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)
  • New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor

TDS Model:

  • 100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"
  • No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts
  • Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now

Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration

  • Current spend: $200K+/month direct with AWS
  • Aptum led migration from $0 → $50K+/month using MAP funding
  • Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions
  • Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

 

Action Items

Aptum (@Matthew Carter / @Marc Pare):

  1. Review current DSA for 90-day notification clauses—URGENT if moving
  2. Discuss billing transfer impact with customer (automation implications)
  3. Complete due diligence with other distributors

TDS (@Rupesh):

  • Provide DSA transfer procedures once Aptum decides

 

Important Notes

Timing Considerations:

  • April 1st program changes happen regardless of distributor choice
  • DSA transfer can complete within hours (pending agreement terms)
  • 90-day notification clause could delay April 1st implementation
  • Partner Growth Incentive lost for one year when switching distributors

Current Limitations:

  • AWS hasn't released final program documentation (expected in "couple more weeks")
  • TDS cannot provide detailed deal support until DSA established (competitive boundary)

 

Next Steps

  1. Aptum completes distributor evaluation
  2. If moving to TDS: Initiate DSA transfer process
  3. Schedule technical deep dive (post-decision)
  4. Customer billing transfer discussion (post-DSA completion)

Hello Aptum team,

Nice to e-meet you all.

Thank you for your due diligence, you’re asking all the right questions. I did my best to respond succinctly in email, but I’d love to walk through each point in more depth on a call, especially if you’re interested in how we differentiate compared to other AWS distributors.

  • Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
    • Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
    • StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
    • In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
    • If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
  • Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
    • Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
    • The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation.
    • We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
  • API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
    • Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
  • What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
    • Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
    • Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
      Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
      Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.
  • Will we be assigned a dedicated account manager, technical expert or help with pre-sales and design? (saving strategies and rightsizing recommendations are critical).
    • Yes, you’ll have a dedicated AWS account manager, plus access to technical SMEs and pre-sales engineering resources.
    • Given the breadth of AWS service delivery designations and competencies (e.g., MSP, migrations, government, education, to name a few), our AWS practice provides technical expertise and support across migrations & modernization, managed services, professional services, assessments, and AWS workloads.
    • Additionally, our ServiceSolv team provides engineering, integration, deployment, and remediation services which enables you to offer end-to-end customer lifecycle support without building these functions internally. We can also provide white-labeled resources delivered under your branding, where applicable.
  • Net 90 terms
    • Extended payment terms are feasible through TD SYNNEX. We'd have to get you in contact with our credit/finance team to review your eligibility

HI Marc,

 

Please find below the answers to your questions.

 

We have clients that are using AWS native tools for cost reporting and we are keen to understand when/what changes with their experience.

 

From your customer’s perspective, there should be no change to their experience when it comes to using AWS native cost reporting tools. Services such as Cost Explorer, CUR (Cost and Usage Reports), and other AWS-native billing and reporting capabilities will continue to function exactly as they do today.The transition to TD SYNNEX as the distribution partner primarily affects the billing and distribution relationship on the backend, not the operational environment within AWS.  

 

We are wondering if there is a two hop route to implementing this change and what the impact would be to our margin. Phase  1) just make TD our distribution , Phase 2) move some customers over to new model that removes Root for Distribution. 

 

Now, there isn’t a simple or automatic way to transfer accounts between different distribution models. While initiating the transition to TD SYNNEX as the distributor is certainly possible, moving accounts across structural models typically requires careful coordination and review on a case-by-case basis to ensure the transition is handled smoothly.

I would also recommend reviewing the exit clause with your current distributor. In most cases, partners are required to provide a 90-day notice period before making a move to another distributor. Ensuring this is aligned early in the process helps avoid any delays or contractual complications during the transition.

 

Kindly revert on any clarifications.

 

Thanks & Regards

 

Rupesh Sankar

Business Development Manager

TD SYNNEX

Mississauga, ON, L5N 8G1

Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Marketplace

 

Hi Christine, Gina & Rupesh

Please treat as priority and address comments in blue:

 

  • Custom cost-management tools and Security dashboards via StreamOne Ion?
    • Yes, the StreamOne platform provides pre-built dashboards and the ability to create custom reports, including configurable alerts. These reports can show usage, consumption trends, and other financial insights.
    • StreamOne also offers real-time reporting on IaaS consumption (Azure, AWS, and Google), enabling advanced cost-optimization capabilities and continuous billing visibility, not just month-end reporting.
    • In addition, StreamOne includes a SecOps Portal for partners, providing a centralized dashboard to view and manage customer security features and policies.
    • If you need more specific details, I’m happy to follow up.
    • Please provide detailed steps how you will assist us with the change-over from another aggregator to TDS?
  • Access to AWS funding programs, ACE deal registrations and leads?
    • Yes, As a Premier Tier AWS Partner, we help our partners leverage AWS mechanisms and programs, including offerings, funding, enablement, and guidance on ACE deal registrations as part of co-sell execution (where eligible and aligned to AWS criteria).
    • The TD SYNNEX AWS team also has a dedicated Customer Success and Conversion team focused on customer lead generation and demand generation. Who exactly would be part of our team? Where does Gina fit into the picture and how can we utilize her as a resource?
    • We’ve built an infrastructure framework around programs like SBAI and other third-party lead-gen mechanisms to maintain, hydrate, nurture, and route end-customer leads to our partners—across both Commercial and Public Sector.
  • API access or automated insights for billing data independent of AWS (i.e., aligning payment terms, managing outstanding balances are important to us)
    • Yes, both are available. I can connect you with the team that managed this process so they can provide a detailed overview and answer any specific questions.
  • What GTM strategies do you offer? Free training and discounted workshops? Co-sponsored AWS events?
    • Yes, TD SYNNEX offers several GTM motions to help partners build and scale pipeline, including enablement, co-marketing, and co-sell support.
    • Pipeline: Top-of-funnel, assessment-led sales plays for customers both on AWS and not yet on AWS.
      Training: Fully subsidized training and enablement programs are available.
      Events: Depending on the use case, eligibility, and cost, we can either fully subsidize or co-invest in joint webinars, SKOs, and customer-facing events—provided there’s a defined solution focus, target audience, pipeline target, and measurable ROI.

Aptum is evaluating moving their AWS distribution partnership to TDS to address customer visibility concerns and improve margin structure. Key opportunity: $2M from a $5M AWS customer account, targeting 6% margins with 3% customer pass-through.

Current Situation

April 1st AWS Program Changes

Major structural changes:

  • Partners receive full 8% front-end discount (up from 3%)
  • Distributors move to back-end rebate only (no front-end discount)
  • New billing transfer program creates visibility silos between customer/partner/distributor

TDS Model:

  • 100% pass-through: "You get what you earn"
  • No tiered programs—partners receive full earned discounts
  • Root exception & billing transfer capabilities ready now

Additional Opportunity: Fintech Customer Migration

  • Current spend: $200K+/month direct with AWS
  • Aptum led migration from $0 → $50K+/month using MAP funding
  • Opportunity: TDS can build exception case based on historical migration work and APFP submissions
  • Billing transfer allows account capture while customer retains root access

Important Notes

  • DSA transfer can complete within hours (pending agreement terms)

If DB wants it per customer's MRR,   I think utilizing the profitability data Jorge put together will be best.  And contribution margin would be Revenues less any direct costs associated with customer,  excluding rent and other overheads that isnt captured by Jorge.   


Dennis Lam
Manager, Finance

+1 (604) 909-6386
aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 1:44 PM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: information request - DB
 

Hey Team,

 

We do have a request coming in for updated information for Digital Bridge. For each of the below please indicate timing to which you can provide the requested information if you can’t provide it by EOD tomorrow. I tagged who can provide the info to the best of my knowledge, but feel free to collaborate on these.

 

  • Details on top 10 clients, including MRR (@Paul Billard), scope (what LOB @Paul Billard), contract expiry (@Adam Nelson and @Kathy Wu or @Tom Kelsch) and margin  (For margin let’s use the contribution margin here please and add a note to what is the contribution margin (@Paul Billard and @Dennis Lam)
    1. Also include customer demographics (e.g. Industry and region) @Paul Billard and @Tom Kelsch please collaborate on this
  • % of revenue on contract vs month-to-month and average remaining contract term @Paul Billard you can leverage the Run Rate file and the renewal tracker.
  • Current booked backlog and sales pipeline @Tom Kelsch please get us the pipeline by MRC versus NRC and the LOB, TCV etc. Excel format please. @Kathy Wu@Paul Billard current booked backlog would be all closed won opportunities for MRC and NRC and what is left on the contract or left to be delivered on. Again the renewal tracker can be useful here.
  • Historical capex outlay for previous 3-5 years for maintenance, success-based capex and any facilities investments (will require any capex we financed via capital lease as I requested be added to the historical master file  @Dennis Lam
    1. Expected capex outlay required over next 3 years & for physical assets – age, expected life, refresh plans, investments required to maintain @George Revie
  • Remaining DC lease terms by site (pro forma for lease renewals) @George Revie

 

Please let me know of any questions.

 

 

Sarah Blanchard

VP, Finance

+1 (514) 673-7582
aptum.com | LinkedIn

body.content:

Subject: Re: Triple Jump - Contributor access in Azure for support assessment and pre-migration check
 
No, my understanding is that this would just be a mechanism to move their subscriptions to us, not a deeper review of how it’s all been deployed. 

So there’s still potential for a WAR. 

Darren Wells
Senior Strategic Account Manager

+44 7852 926026
aptum.com | LinkedIn

So this is an approach instead of the well architected review?



Frederic Gingras
Senior Director, Account Management

m: +1 514-913-9153 teams: +1 514-673-7553
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Sent: March 17, 2026 6:44 AM
To: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: FW: Triple Jump - Contributor access in Azure for support assessment and pre-migration check
 
FYI ref one of the fronts we’re pursuing with Triple Jump...

Darren Wells
Senior Strategic Account Manager

+44 7852 926026
aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Andy Petterson <Andy.Petterson@aptum.com>
Date: Wednesday, 4 March 2026 at 13:14
To: Tal Baron <tal@triplejumptech.com>
Cc: Darren Wells <Darren.Wells@aptum.com>, Robert Grafton <Robert.Grafton@aptum.com>
Subject: Contributor access in Azure for support assessment and pre-migration check

Hi Tal,

Hope you're well and the move to SQL Standard has been successful, please let us know if you need any more assistance with it.

I know Darren is currently looking at possibly migrating your Azure subscription from Microsoft to our CSP account so that you guys can benefit from our Microsoft Premier Partner Support agreement, and adding our management wrap so that we can monitor and react to issues in your environment on your behalf.

For us to better understand how easy it would be to migrate your subscription over, and to review what services you're running, would it be possible to set Rob up with a contributor account so he can login and take a look? You can either invite him as a guest with his email address, or set up a generic Aptum account in your tenant for us to use. Rob can assist with this if needed.

Once we have access we should be able to login, assess the environment and then let you know what a migration would look like, and the associated costs with adding our MCP support wrap.

Regards,

Andy Petterson
Solutions Architect

+442380926091
aptum.com | LinkedIn

data.subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

body.content:
Hi Rick,

Thanks for your collaboration here, again, we’re only porting US customers, but will remain with Ingram for the UK and Canadian regions.

 

On that note, can you confirm steps to ensure we’ll receive the 8% discount in those geographies effective 4/1?

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Marshbanks, Rick <Rick.Marshbanks@ingrammicro.com>
Sent: February 18, 2026 5:33 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Cc: Horwath, John <John.Horwath@ingrammicro.com>; Olvera, Israel <Israel.Olvera2@ingrammicro.com>; Marc Pare <marc.pare@aptum.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>; Jennings, Adam <Adam.Jennings@ingrammicro.com>; Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita <Lalita.Jaroenchonwanit@ingrammicro.com>; Chandrashekar, Subramanya <Subramanya.Chandrashekar@ingrammicro.com>; Adam Nelson <adam.nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

+@Chandrashekar, Subramanya / @Jaroenchonwanit, Lalita

 

Hi Matt, Thank you for the note and for the partnership we have built together. While I am disappointed to hear we will not be with you for the next phase of your AWS Partner Journey, I sincerely appreciate the collaboration and trust Aptum has placed in Ingram Micro throughout our relationship. Given the April 1 transition timeline, I welcome the opportunity to schedule a brief call with the broader team to review the offboarding process to help ensure everything runs smoothly. This would also allow us to align on any country-specific considerations and required documentation so there are no disruptions.

 

I would also value the opportunity to better understand the factors that led up to this decision. If there are areas where we could have delivered more value, I want to ensure we take that feedback constructively as we continue to improve and adapt as a global business unit.  If you prefer, I am happy to connect one-on-one as well. Please let me know what works best for you and your team.

 

Regardless of the path forward, we remain committed to supporting Aptum through a seamless transition and keeping the door open for future collaboration.

 

Respectfully, RM

 

Rick Marshbanks

AWS Business Development Executive

St Petersburg, FL

(248) 622.0869

 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Friday, February 13, 2026 4:03 PM
To: AWS Cloud Operations <awscloudops@ingrammicro.com>; Rosario, Jonathan <Jonathan.Rosario@ingrammicro.com>
Cc: Marc Pare <Marc.Pare@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: [EXTERNAL]Formal Notice of AWS Distribution Agreement Change

 

Dear Ingram Micro Team,

Please accept this message as notice that we intend to transition our AWS DSA to another aggregator effective April 1.

 

We appreciate the support and partnership we have with Ingram Micro and thank you for your assistance throughout our relationship.

 

We will circle back closer to April with regards to next steps and documentation needed to complete this transition.

Thanks,

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Hi Adam, 

Saw you lead service delivery at Aptum and work closely with customers running cloud and infrastructure environments.

A lot of teams in that position are getting asked for more flexibility around where workloads run and what platforms they support.

We’ve been talking with teams exploring a second cloud option alongside what they already offer.

Open to learning more?

Cheers,
Drew

Strategic Sales Development Representative

data.subject: Your CRM Is Holding Back Your Revenue

body.content:
Website →

Most CRMs were built to store contacts, not to create revenue.

That’s why so many teams find themselves exporting data, juggling multiple tools, and spending hours trying to turn static

contact lists into real conversations.


Your CRM becomes a database — not a growth engine.

Contact Sales

Contact Science changes that.

Instead of forcing your team to manage contacts manually, Contact Science transforms your prospecting and marketing into a data-driven system designed to create opportunities automatically.


With Contact Science, you can:

  • Discover and identify high-value prospects with precision

  • Launch targeted outreach campaigns in minutes

  • Track engagement and intent signals in real time

  • Automatically prioritize the prospects most likely to convert

  • Turn marketing data directly into qualified pipeline

Just clean data, intelligent targeting, and faster

pipeline growth.

Smarter Prospecting

  • Identify and prioritize the right accounts automatically

  • Build custom campaigns that cut through the noise, utilize multi-touch outreach

  • Equip sales with actionable insights, not just contact records

  • Give managers real-time visibility into every conversation and conversion

Learn more

Marketing That Actually Converts

  • Customizable templates to align campaigns to target market

  • Improve segmentation using real engagement signals

  • Close the loop between outreach, engagement, and revenue

  • Custom workflows, automated campaigns including Post Card mailings, and personalized nurture sequences that streamline outreach and follow-up.

Learn more

User-Friendly Design

  • Clean, intuitive interface with minimal learning curve

  • Automatically pulls leads from website forms with real time alerts

  • Flexible, reporting dashboards, integrations, and pricing - month to month

  • Free training, onboarding help, and ongoing support so the system actually works with you.

  • Grows with your needs, integrates easily with other tools, and delivers real results.

Learn more

Let’s build a process that works for you!

Fast onboarding means you can start generating value from day one. Our team handles the heavy lifting to get your data, workflows, and campaigns up and running quickly - so there’s no long implementation period or complicated setup. 


Every customer also receives free live training to ensure your team knows exactly how to use Contact Science to its full potential. 


And if you ever need help, our live support team is readily available, providing real humans who can answer questions, troubleshoot issues, and help you keep your prospecting and marketing running smoothly.


Get started today!

Schedule a Demo

www.contactscience.com

214-483-5800

Hey Team,

  • Details on top 10 clients, including MRR (@Paul Billard), scope (what LOB @Paul Billard), contract expiry (@Adam Nelson and @Kathy Wu or @Tom Kelsch) and margin  (For margin let’s use the contribution margin here please and add a note to what is the contribution margin (@Paul Billard and @Dennis Lam)

  • % of revenue on contract vs month-to-month and average remaining contract term @Paul Billard you can leverage the Run Rate file and the renewal tracker.
  • Current booked backlog and sales pipeline @Tom Kelsch please get us the pipeline by MRC versus NRC and the LOB, TCV etc. Excel format please. @Kathy Wu@Paul Billard current booked backlog would be all closed won opportunities for MRC and NRC and what is left on the contract or left to be delivered on. Again the renewal tracker can be useful here.
  • Historical capex outlay for previous 3-5 years for maintenance, success-based capex and any facilities investments (will require any capex we financed via capital lease as I requested be added to the historical master file  @Dennis Lam
    1. Expected capex outlay required over next 3 years & for physical assets – age, expected life, refresh plans, investments required to maintain @George Revie
  • Remaining DC lease terms by site (pro forma for lease renewals) @George Revie

 

Please let me know of any questions.

 

 

Sarah Blanchard

VP, Finance

aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 4:35:55 PM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: eBOARDs Margin Recovery on Future Orders

 

Hi Tom, Kathy and Roger,

Gentle reminder as we approach the eBoards renewal, to bake/recover +U$2K/mo as we’re losing money on Imperva.

 

Thanks,

 

Matthew Carter

VP, Project Management & Global Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: September 11, 2025 4:43 PM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: re: eBOARDs Margin Recovery on Future Orders

 

Hi Tom & Kathy,

Any future upgrades or new orders for this customer must carry strong margins to offset the losses on the Imperva product. We’re targeting a recovery of +U$2K/mo.

eBoards has upcoming MCP and PenTest requirements, be sure to blend additional margins and let me know if we secure any deals.

 

Hi Roger,

As discussed, if we’re unable to offset margins, we need to revisit contracts (next year) to either a) increase rates or b) offer an alternative strategy (CloudFlare or other).

Thanks,

 

Notes:

  • eBoards Imperva margins 8%, our target is 35%
  • Good customer relationship, but lots of volatility towards Imperva product. Price disruption will risk churn.  
  • eBoards has 3 Imperva contracts: $1K Sep ’26, $3K Nov ‘26 and $5K Oct ’27 (commercially, we could only increase the last contract).
  • Our monthly Imperva cost $7.8K vs. eBoards billing $8.4K

 

 

Matthew Carter

Senior Global Director, Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, September 9, 2025 2:42 PM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Hi Matt,

 

I am surprised to hear that the overall margin is so poor - on this recently approved Deal Desk request the markup was much higher than normal for a 3rd party resale.

 

Here's how I worked through it:

 

  • I reviewed the pricing and costs for the client's existing Imperva bot protection service:
    • Costs: $3,936 USD per month (first quote attached)
      • $2,027 for 100M requests
      • $1,732 for 5 hours monthly support
    • Price: $5,314 USD per month
    • Markup: 35% ($5,314 / $3,936)
  • And then the figures on the request for increased service:
    • Costs: $2,534 per month for 125M requests (second quote attached - it shows $30,404 for 12 months but I understand we pay monthly, so $2,534 per month)
      • Increase of $507 per month (from $2,027 for 100M requests)
      • As per Roger there are no change to support hours or cost
    • Price: $1,329 MRC increase from existing rate
    • Markup: 262% ($1,329 / $507)

 

The reason the calculated markup on the incremental new service is so high is because Roger proposed simply taking the current monthly rate of $5,314 (which includes the unchanged support) and increasing it by 25%, proportional to the increase in protection requests (100M to 125M). $5,314 x 1.25 = $1,329. I approved this is it is well above our usual markup for 3rd party resale.

 

Hopefully the above makes sense - please let me know if I have missed anything. It was not my intention to provide any favorable discounting for a client that does not meet profitability targets.

 

Thanks,

 

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst

aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, September 9, 2025 6:24 AM
To: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>
Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: FW: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Hi Tom,

Let’s sample this recent order: What is the mark-up on this product and how does your process work?

 

Matthew Carter

Senior Global Director, Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

 

 

 

From: Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, September 9, 2025 9:00 AM

Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Sam Carter <Sam.Carter@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Well, that is interesting because there is no way for me to get an Imperva product through DD with an 8% uplift. When we get the quotes from Imperva, I send it to DD and they add the 35%... that is how it has always worked.

 

I would be interested to see how there is only 8%....

 

Thanks Matt!

 

Roger Gonzales

Account Manager

+1 (210.243.3807)
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, September 8, 2025 7:12 PM
To: Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Sam Carter <Sam.Carter@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>; Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Hi Roger,

Glad we increased their rate, but unfortunately, it’s not enough. We target a 35% margin on Imperva products and eBoards is currently at 8%. With overhead, we’re likely losing money.

 

I’ll set time in your calendar to discuss strategy.

Thanks

 

Matthew Carter

Senior Global Director, Supply Chain

+1 (416) 847-0852
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, September 8, 2025 11:47 AM
To: Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>; Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>
Cc: Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Noe Ibarra <Noe.Ibarra@aptum.com>; Sam Carter <Sam.Carter@aptum.com>; Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: RE: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Well, this customer just renewed their WAF and they are paying $1150 more than they were paying due to an add on called Advanced Bot Protection. Here is the agreement…

 

I would not recommend us raising the pricing on this customer….. he is on the precipice of leaving us due to all of the little issues he has have and changes he has seen.

 

Roger Gonzales

Account Manager

+1 (210.243.3807)
aptum.com | LinkedIn

From: Carlton Currey <Carlton.Currey@aptum.com>
Sent: Tuesday, September 2, 2025 9:39 AM
To: Matthew Carter <Matthew.Carter@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Roger Gonzales <Roger.Gonzales@aptum.com>

Cc: Frederic Gingras <Frederic.Gingras@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Deal Desk Has Approved: eBOARDs - Imperva Advanced Bot Protection Upgrade

 

Hi Matt,

 

I am surprised to hear that the overall margin is so poor - on this recently approved Deal Desk request the markup was much higher than normal for a 3rd party resale.

 

Here's how I worked through it:

 

  • I reviewed the pricing and costs for the client's existing Imperva bot protection service:
    • Costs: $3,936 USD per month (first quote attached)
      • $2,027 for 100M requests
      • $1,732 for 5 hours monthly support
    • Price: $5,314 USD per month
    • Markup: 35% ($5,314 / $3,936)
  • And then the figures on the request for increased service:
    • Costs: $2,534 per month for 125M requests (second quote attached - it shows $30,404 for 12 months but I understand we pay monthly, so $2,534 per month)
      • Increase of $507 per month (from $2,027 for 100M requests)
      • As per Roger there are no change to support hours or cost
    • Price: $1,329 MRC increase from existing rate
    • Markup: 262% ($1,329 / $507)

 

The reason the calculated markup on the incremental new service is so high is because Roger proposed simply taking the current monthly rate of $5,314 (which includes the unchanged support) and increasing it by 25%, proportional to the increase in protection requests (100M to 125M). $5,314 x 1.25 = $1,329. I approved this is it is well above our usual markup for 3rd party resale.

 

Hopefully the above makes sense - please let me know if I have missed anything. It was not my intention to provide any favorable discounting for a client that does not meet profitability targets.

 

Thanks,

 

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst

Hi Matt,

 

I am surprised to hear that the overall margin is so poor - on this recently approved Deal Desk request the markup was much higher than normal for a 3rd party resale.

 

Here's how I worked through it:

 

  • I reviewed the pricing and costs for the client's existing Imperva bot protection service:
    • Costs: $3,936 USD per month (first quote attached)
      • $2,027 for 100M requests
      • $1,732 for 5 hours monthly support
    • Price: $5,314 USD per month
    • Markup: 35% ($5,314 / $3,936)
  • And then the figures on the request for increased service:
    • Costs: $2,534 per month for 125M requests (second quote attached - it shows $30,404 for 12 months but I understand we pay monthly, so $2,534 per month)
      • Increase of $507 per month (from $2,027 for 100M requests)
      • As per Roger there are no change to support hours or cost
    • Price: $1,329 MRC increase from existing rate
    • Markup: 262% ($1,329 / $507)

 

The reason the calculated markup on the incremental new service is so high is because Roger proposed simply taking the current monthly rate of $5,314 (which includes the unchanged support) and increasing it by 25%, proportional to the increase in protection requests (100M to 125M). $5,314 x 1.25 = $1,329. I approved this is it is well above our usual markup for 3rd party resale.

 

Hopefully the above makes sense - please let me know if I have missed anything. It was not my intention to provide any favorable discounting for a client that does not meet profitability targets.

 

Thanks,

 

Tom Kelsch

Senior Decision Support Analyst

data.subject: [Webinaire] Voir, comprendre et protéger l’ensemble de vos actifs numériques : IT, IoT, IoMT, OT

body.content:
You don't often get email from evenementslesaffaires@groupecontex.ca. Learn why this is important

[GRATUIT] Inscrivez-vous au webinaire du 24 mars prochain!

 
Si vous avez des difficultés à lire ce courriel cliquez ici
 
 
 – Webinaire Gratuit –


Présenté par 		 
 
   
 
 24 mars de 12h à 13h  
 
 Voir, comprendre et protéger l’ensemble de vos actifs
numériques : IT, IoT, IoMT, OT 		 
 
 À mesure que les organisations multiplient les environnements connectés, la surface d’attaque s’élargit et les angles morts de sécurité se multiplient. La simple visibilité des actifs ne suffit plus : elle doit se traduire par une protection cohérente et continue.

Ce webinaire propose une lecture structurée des enjeux liés à la gestion des actifs IT, IoT, IoMT et OT. À travers l’approche de la plateforme Forescout 4D Platform™, les participants verront comment identifier les actifs, évaluer les risques, automatiser les réponses et instaurer une gouvernance de sécurité adaptée, tant en environnements locaux que cloud.
 
 
JE M’INSCRIS AU WEBINAIRE
 
 
 
Alban Delagne
Ingénieur avant-vente
FORESCOUT
Vincent Turmel
Ingénieur avant-vente, Spécialiste TO
FORESCOUT
 
 
   
 
 3 bonnes raisons de participer à ce webinaire :
 
 
1
Renforcez la résilience de vos environnements face aux cybermenaces
2
Transformez la visibilité en protection concrète et mesurable
3
Évaluez les bénéfices d’une approche unifiée IT, IoT et OT
 
 
 
EN SAVOIR PLUS

data.subject: Meet the Frontier Suite: Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365 are here

body.content:
Plus: Explore the latest renewal resources, incentives, and skilling.

Having trouble viewing this email? | View as a web page
 
 
 

Introducing Microsoft 365 E7—the productivity suite for Frontier Transformation

 
We’re equipping partners and customers with new capabilities to advance Frontier Transformation—grounded in intelligence and trust.
 
Read more 
 

On March 9, we introduced the Frontier Suite called Microsoft 365 E7, with general availability of Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365—the control plane for agents that enables partners and customers to manage and secure agents with confidence.

Microsoft 365 E7 is the productivity suite for a human‑led, agent‑operated enterprise, turning human intent into action with AI that runs securely at scale. Microsoft 365 E7 brings together Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and built-in governance through Agent 365—creating a powerful opportunity for partners to drive successful AI deployments and adoption.

During the month of March, there will be several opportunities to gain insight into this new announcement. You are encouraged to participate in the Agent 365 "Ask Microsoft Anything" session on March 18 and to register for the Digital Partner Airlift on March 31 to further your understanding of the Agentic System of Work.

To accelerate your Microsoft 365 E5 to E7 upsell and Agent 365 purchase conversations, we've made two updates to further support customer engagements through Copilot + Power Accelerate starting April 1:

  • Agent 365 is now included in the scope of Immersion Briefings, Envisioning and proof of concept (PoC) engagements, and Deployment Accelerators.
  • Performance measurements have been updated to provide partners with credit for incremental customer revenue through Agent 365 and E7 SKUs (Microsoft 365 Copilot is a component of E7 only).

Starting April 1, 2026, Agent 365 and E7 will also be included as eligible workloads for the core, tier 2 strategic product accelerator, and growth levers in Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) incentives.

Renewal resources for Copilot and AI Business Solutions

Tap into the latest Microsoft 365 and Copilot offers and resources—plus expanded investments—to strengthen your renewal and upsell motions, close deals faster, and turn AI experimentation into real business outcomes for more customers.

  • Explore what’s new in AI Business Solutions for partners—covering the latest announcements, partner incentives and offers, skilling and events, and go-to-market campaigns.
  • Increase licensing coverage and build momentum beyond pilots with a new limited-time 30% promotion on Microsoft 365 Copilot that complements existing 15% and 20% promotions.
  • Equip sellers to confidently lead customer conversations with activation kits for Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Chat, and agents.
  • Activate opportunities with the new extra-small engagement size for Cloud Endpoints Envisioning and PoC engagements for customers with more than 500 seats of Windows 11 Enterprise and Microsoft Intune and more than 40% Intune usage. Learn more in the incentives guide.
  • Offer month-to-month billing flexibility for Microsoft 365 Copilot Business and respective bundles as of March 1, 2026.
  • Close more deals with extended promotion terms for Copilot Business offers—including new bundles for customers with less than 300 seats—through June 30, 2026.
  • Leverage new partner profitability resources to maximize margins, incentives, and deal profitability with Copilot Business bundles.

Scale with the Security opportunity

Explore what’s new in Microsoft Security for partners—covering the latest announcements, partner incentives and offers, skilling and events, and go-to-market campaigns.

Fuel your pipeline at RSAC 2026

RSAC™ 2026 is a high‑impact moment for Microsoft Security partners to engage customers and Microsoft leaders around today’s most urgent challenges: securing AI, modernizing identity, and protecting data at scale.

Microsoft Security partners are invited to kick off RSAC 2026 early at the Microsoft Security Pre-Day Experience on March 22, featuring executive insights on AI threats, emerging trends, and the future of security—plus networking, a keynote, and an evening reception. In addition, on March 23, join our Security Reborn in the Era of AI workshop for actionable guidance on building your Security Copilot agent and publishing it on the new Microsoft Security Store.

Join the Advanced Security Growth Engine community call on March 25

Learn how partners can accelerate growth using ASPX (AI Business Solutions and Security Insights)—our data‑driven targeting platform—to identify the right customers, drive adoption, and deliver measurable results. Get practical insights to sharpen your go‑to‑market strategy and strengthen your security business. Register now:

  • 8:00–9:00 AM Pacific Time (Americas/EMEA)
  • 7:00–8:00 PM Pacific Time (Asia)

Now in public preview: Microsoft Security Dashboard for AI

With the new Security Dashboard for AI, you can give customers a unified, real-time view of AI assets and risk signals across Microsoft Defender, Entra, and Purview.

Easily deliver AI security assessments, governance planning, and remediation services for customers—and serve as a trusted advisor as they scale, secure, and govern AI across agents, apps, and platforms.

New Cyber Pulse report shows governance and security are shaping the new AI frontier

AI agents are expanding quickly, with more than 80% of the Fortune 500 already running agents built with low‑code or no‑code tools—and customers need stronger observability and governance to manage how these agents access data and work across their environments.

Read the Cyber Pulse report for a clear view of the latest cybersecurity risks and practical guidance for making risk management your competitive advantage.

Unlock new revenue with attach SKUs for Microsoft Defender Suite and Microsoft Purview Suite

Confidently lead your attach and renewal conversations with the Security Business Case Builder—which includes the Defender Suite and Purview Suite attach SKUs for Microsoft 365 Business Premium, licensing paths, and Forrester-backed ROI insights—to show customers how they can strengthen security and reduce costs.

Issue update: Business Premium + Copilot bundle prerequisite validation error

The Business Premium + Copilot bundle wasn't consistently recognized as meeting the prerequisite for the Advanced Security add-on SKUs in Partner Center. This has been resolved as of February 19, and eligible customers can continue to transact the Advanced Security add-on SKUs as expected.

Security incentives news

As of March 1, 2026, we’ve implemented several partner investment updates to simplify requirements, align incentives to customer usage and growth, and strengthen your earning opportunities. Review the latest guidance and on‑demand resources so you can make the most of these updates.

Microsoft Sentinel Accelerator qualification and usage model updates

Microsoft Sentinel Accelerator will shift from a daily ingestion model to a monthly usage model. The qualification, activation, and stabilization criteria will also be updated. Partners will now:

  • Qualify with customers using 0–1,500 GB/month.
  • Earn activation at 1,500 GB/month.
  • Receive increased activation and stabilization payment amounts for a more predictable and growth‑aligned incentive experience.

Updated delivery guidance for Security Immersion Briefings

Threat Protection and Data Security Immersion Briefings will offer updated delivery guidance and refreshed resources—including new briefing kits for both Microsoft 365 E3 and Business Premium customers—to make it easier for you to deliver high‑value, one‑to‑many Security sessions and build customer intent.

The Advanced Security for Business Premium Immersion Briefing is not a standalone engagement and must be nominated through Threat Protection or Data Security Immersion Briefings.

Security Envisioning Workshops performance measure changes

The Security Portfolio Performance Measure has been reduced from 33% to 15%. Partners must achieve at least a 15% performance rate across all completed Security engagements to remain eligible.

While the requirement is now 15%, consideration for earning cap extensions still requires delivering performance on at least 33% of engagements. Earning cap extensions are subject to budget availability.

Learn more in the incentives guide.

TECHNICAL CHANGES AND REMINDERS

Final reminder: MFA enforcement for Partner Center APIs begins April 1

On April 1, 2026, multifactor authentication (MFA) enforcement for all Partner Center app+user APIs will begin. Any API calls made without MFA after this date will be blocked. If you haven't already, update your systems now to maintain uninterrupted operations.

Learn more about MFA enforcement for Partner Center APIs.

Reminder: Extended service terms enforcement begins May 4

Effective May 4, 2026, the free grace period for accessing services on non-renewed subscriptions will be discontinued. Customers who don't renew must either cancel at end of term and lose access immediately or enter a paid extended service term (EST) to continue service. The extended service term bills monthly at the current monthly term rate plus a 3% uplift (or 23% if no monthly plan exists).

As of February 27, 2026, partners can export a list of subscriptions that are scheduled to move to EST at end of term for standard offers so they can proactively review and align end-of-term decisions with customers.

Learn more about EST, timelines, available resources, and how to export a list of EST subscriptions.

SKILLING

Win more customers—and revenue—with a certification

Microsoft internal data shows that when partners combine certifications with hands-on training, they consistently outperform peers across Microsoft solution areas, achieving faster deal velocity, stronger pipeline conversion, and higher margins.

Ready to turn your skills into credibility and revenue?

Partners can register for an upcoming Certification Week to get credential-ready with hands-on labs, exam prep, and expert-led training. Delivered regionally live or on demand, Certification Weeks offer comprehensive training—with new Sales tracks—across Cloud & AI Platforms, Security, and AI Business Solutions.

More skilling resources to move your business forward

  • Visit the skilling resources page on the Partner Skilling Hub to discover the latest event calendars, playbooks, and updates—and register for training aligned to your business priorities.
  • Build confidence on your schedule with on-demand certification prep on LevelUp.
  • Contact AskSkilling with any questions.

LevelUp CSP: Win with Microsoft Security

Empower your customers with the value of Microsoft Security. Join us for a reoccurring digital training series designed to help CSP partners confidently position Microsoft Security solutions. Each session alternates between two key focus areas—Data Security and Threat Protection—to build partner expertise across the full Microsoft Security portfolio. The next event is scheduled for Monday, March 18. Learn more and register: Americas, EMEA, Asia.

  • Learn about Microsoft Security licensing and Defender and Purview Suites.
  • Access go-to-market resources, demos, and skilling paths.
  • Discover current promotions and incentives to activate.
  • Get actionable guidance from Microsoft Security sales experts during a live Q&A.

ANNOUNCEMENTS

Everything you need to upskill and drive demand, in one easy-to-use kit

Simplify and speed up execution across Stage 1 of the Microsoft Customer Engagement Methodology with a Partner Success Kit—complete with skilling resources to enable your team and go‑to‑market tools to launch campaigns faster.

Protect customer trust with fraud prevention and resilience

Customer fraud is rising globally, and CSP partners are ultimately accountable for fraudulent purchases and nonpayment. Recognizing common fraud patterns early—and following consistent onboarding and escalation processes—can help protect your revenue and reputation.

Read our latest blog post on recent fraud trends in the ecosystem, platform baselines every partner should meet, and practical fraud prevention strategies.

Share your feedback to shape the future of CSP enablement

Microsoft is pausing the CSP Partner Enablement Series while we take time to redesign the experience with partner input at the center. Please take a few minutes to complete this survey to provide feedback on these community calls.

Windows Server and SQL Server subscription update

On April 1, 2026, Microsoft is updating the product terms for Windows Server and SQL Server subscriptions purchased under the Microsoft Customer Agreement that make those subscriptions eligible for license mobility rights and ensure that subscriptions purchased through CSP have the same benefits as those provided through Software Assurance in our legacy Volume Licensing offers. Review the updated product terms.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Copyright 2026 Microsoft Corporation.

 

You have received this message from Microsoft as a result of your participation in the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program. This message contains important information on your general membership, or your benefits as a member.

 
Privacy Statement
 

Microsoft Corporation
One Microsoft Way
Redmond, WA 98052

data.subject: Dear Adam, invitation to 6th High-Density AI Data Center Infrastructure Summit in Ashburn

body.content:
You don't often get email from andrea@environment-events.com. Learn why this is important
Hello Adam Nelson,
 
I hope this email finds you well. Did you have a chance to check the agenda for the 6th High-Density AI Data Center Infrastructure Summit 2026, which will be held on May 27th-28th in Ashburn, Virginia? The event is co-located with the 7th Data Centre Design, Engineering & Construction Summit. This event is a great opportunity to connect with industry leaders and professionals, explore industry insights, and expand your network.
 
Could you please let me know your thoughts on the upcoming event?
 
We are currently offering special rates for all registrations completed by March 19th. For more information and latest agenda, please refer to the enclosed brochure. If you wish to register and benefit from the special rate, please complete and send me back the form on the last page of the brochure.
 
We are eager to assist and ensure your participation is as impactful as possible.
 
 
Warm regard,
 
Andrea Olsson | Global Marketing Lead
EU infoline: +420 228 887 426
US infoline: +1 (404) 678 8489
 
 
 
 
 
Let us know by replying “Unsubscribe” if you'd like to be removed from our mailing list
