data.subject: Meet the Frontier Suite: Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365 are here

body.content:
Plus: Explore the latest renewal resources, incentives, and skilling.

Having trouble viewing this email? | View as a web page
 
 
 

Introducing Microsoft 365 E7—the productivity suite for Frontier Transformation

 
We’re equipping partners and customers with new capabilities to advance Frontier Transformation—grounded in intelligence and trust.
 
Read more 
 

On March 9, we introduced the Frontier Suite called Microsoft 365 E7, with general availability of Microsoft 365 E7 and Agent 365—the control plane for agents that enables partners and customers to manage and secure agents with confidence.

Microsoft 365 E7 is the productivity suite for a human‑led, agent‑operated enterprise, turning human intent into action with AI that runs securely at scale. Microsoft 365 E7 brings together Microsoft 365 E5, Microsoft 365 Copilot, and built-in governance through Agent 365—creating a powerful opportunity for partners to drive successful AI deployments and adoption.

During the month of March, there will be several opportunities to gain insight into this new announcement. You are encouraged to participate in the Agent 365 "Ask Microsoft Anything" session on March 18 and to register for the Digital Partner Airlift on March 31 to further your understanding of the Agentic System of Work.

To accelerate your Microsoft 365 E5 to E7 upsell and Agent 365 purchase conversations, we've made two updates to further support customer engagements through Copilot + Power Accelerate starting April 1:

  • Agent 365 is now included in the scope of Immersion Briefings, Envisioning and proof of concept (PoC) engagements, and Deployment Accelerators.
  • Performance measurements have been updated to provide partners with credit for incremental customer revenue through Agent 365 and E7 SKUs (Microsoft 365 Copilot is a component of E7 only).

Starting April 1, 2026, Agent 365 and E7 will also be included as eligible workloads for the core, tier 2 strategic product accelerator, and growth levers in Cloud Solution Provider (CSP) incentives.

Renewal resources for Copilot and AI Business Solutions

  • Tap into the latest Microsoft 365 and Copilot offers and resources—plus expanded investments—to strengthen your renewal and upsell motions, close deals faster, and turn AI experimentation into real business outcomes for more customers.
  • Explore what’s new in AI Business Solutions for partners—covering the latest announcements, partner incentives and offers, skilling and events, and go-to-market campaigns.
  • Increase licensing coverage and build momentum beyond pilots with a new limited-time 30% promotion on Microsoft 365 Copilot that complements existing 15% and 20% promotions.
  • Equip sellers to confidently lead customer conversations with activation kits for Microsoft 365 Copilot, Copilot Chat, and agents.
  • Activate opportunities with the new extra-small engagement size for Cloud Endpoints Envisioning and PoC engagements for customers with more than 500 seats of Windows 11 Enterprise and Microsoft Intune and more than 40% Intune usage. Learn more in the incentives guide.
  • Offer month-to-month billing flexibility for Microsoft 365 Copilot Business and respective bundles as of March 1, 2026.
  • Close more deals with extended promotion terms for Copilot Business offers—including new bundles for customers with less than 300 seats—through June 30, 2026.
  • Leverage new partner profitability resources to maximize margins, incentives, and deal profitability with Copilot Business bundles.

Scale with the Security opportunity

Explore what’s new in Microsoft Security for partners—covering the latest announcements, partner incentives and offers, skilling and events, and go-to-market campaigns.

Fuel your pipeline at RSAC 2026

RSAC™ 2026 is a high‑impact moment for Microsoft Security partners to engage customers and Microsoft leaders around today’s most urgent challenges: securing AI, modernizing identity, and protecting data at scale.

Microsoft Security partners are invited to kick off RSAC 2026 early at the Microsoft Security Pre-Day Experience on March, 22, featuring executive insights on AI threats, emerging trends, and the future of security—plus networking, a keynote, and an evening reception. In addition, on March 23, join our Security Reborn in the Era of AI workshop for actionable guidance on building your Security Copilot agent and publishing it on the new Microsoft Security Store.

Join the Advanced Security Growth Engine community call on March 25

Learn how partners can accelerate growth using ASPX (AI Business Solutions and Security Insights)—our data‑driven targeting platform—to identify the right customers, drive adoption, and deliver measurable results. Get practical insights to sharpen your go‑to‑market strategy and strengthen your security business. Register now:

  • 8:00–9:00 AM Pacific Time (Americas/EMEA)
  • 7:00–8:00 PM Pacific Time (Asia)

Now in public preview: Microsoft Security Dashboard for AI

With the new Security Dashboard for AI, you can give customers a unified, real-time view of AI assets and risk signals across Microsoft Defender, Entra, and Purview.

Easily deliver AI security assessments, governance planning, and remediation services for customers—and serve as a trusted advisor as they scale, secure, and govern AI across agents, apps, and platforms.

New Cyber Pulse report shows governance and security are shaping the new AI frontier

AI agents are expanding quickly, with more than 80% of the Fortune 500 already running agents built with low‑code or no‑code tools—and customers need stronger observability and governance to manage how these agents access data and work across their environments.

Read the Cyber Pulse report for a clear view of the latest cybersecurity risks and practical guidance for making risk management your competitive advantage.

Unlock new revenue with attach SKUs for Microsoft Defender Suite and Microsoft Purview Suite

Confidently lead your attach and renewal conversations with the Security Business Case Builder—which includes the Defender Suite and Purview Suite attach SKUs for Microsoft 365 Business Premium, licensing paths, and Forrester-backed ROI insights—to show customers how they can strengthen security and reduce costs.

Issue update: Business Premium + Copilot bundle prerequisite validation error

The Business Premium + Copilot bundle wasn't consistently recognized as meeting the prerequisite for the Advanced Security add-on SKUs in Partner Center. This has been resolved as of February 19, and eligible customers can continue to transact the Advanced Security add-on SKUs as expected.

Security incentives news

As of March 1, 2026, we’ve implemented several partner investment updates to simplify requirements, align incentives to customer usage and growth, and strengthen your earning opportunities. Review the latest guidance and on‑demand resources so you can make the most of these updates.

Microsoft Sentinel Accelerator qualification and usage model updates

Microsoft Sentinel Accelerator will shift from a daily ingestion model to a monthly usage model. The qualification, activation, and stabilization criteria will also be updated. Partners will now:

  • Qualify with customers using 0–1,500 GB/month.
  • Earn activation at 1,500 GB/month.
  • Receive increased activation and stabilization payment amounts for a more predictable and growth‑aligned incentive experience.

Updated delivery guidance for Security Immersion Briefings

Threat Protection and Data Security Immersion Briefings will offer updated delivery guidance and refreshed resources—including new briefing kits for both Microsoft 365 E3 and Business Premium customers—to make it easier for you to deliver high‑value, one‑to‑many Security sessions and build customer intent.

The Advanced Security for Business Premium Immersion Briefing is not a standalone engagement and must be nominated through Threat Protection or Data Security Immersion Briefings.

Security Envisioning Workshops performance measure changes

The Security Portfolio Performance Measure has been reduced from 33% to 15%. Partners must achieve at least a 15% performance rate across all completed Security engagements to remain eligible.

While the requirement is now 15%, consideration for earning cap extensions still requires delivering performance on at least 33% of engagements. Earning cap extensions are subject to budget availability.

Learn more in the incentives guide.

TECHNICAL CHANGES AND REMINDERS

Final reminder: MFA enforcement for Partner Center APIs begins April 1

On April 1, 2026, multifactor authentication (MFA) enforcement for all Partner Center app+user APIs will begin. Any API calls made without MFA after this date will be blocked. If you haven't already, update your systems now to maintain uninterrupted operations.

Learn more about MFA enforcement for Partner Center APIs.

Reminder: Extended service terms enforcement begins May 4

Effective May 4, 2026, the free grace period for accessing services on non-renewed subscriptions will be discontinued. Customers who don't renew must either cancel at end of term and lose access immediately or enter a paid extended service term (EST) to continue service. The extended service term bills monthly at the current monthly term rate plus a 3% uplift (or 23% if no monthly plan exists).

As of February 27, 2026, partners can export a list of subscriptions that are scheduled to move to EST at end of term for standard offers so they can proactively review and align end-of-term decisions with customers.

Learn more about EST, timelines, available resources, and how to export a list of EST subscriptions.

SKILLING

Win more customers—and revenue—with a certification

Microsoft internal data shows that when partners combine certifications with hands-on training, they consistently outperform peers across Microsoft solution areas, achieving faster deal velocity, stronger pipeline conversion, and higher margins.

Ready to turn your skills into credibility and revenue?

Register for an upcoming Certification Week to get credential-ready with hands-on labs, exam prep, and expert-led training. Delivered regionally live or on demand, Certification Weeks offer comprehensive training—with new Sales tracks—across Cloud & AI Platforms, Security, and AI Business Solutions.

More skilling resources to move your business forward

  • Visit the skilling resources page on the Partner Skilling Hub to discover the latest event calendars, playbooks, and updates—and register for training aligned to your business priorities.
  • Build confidence on your schedule with on-demand certification prep on LevelUp.
  • Contact AskSkilling for any questions.

LevelUp CSP: Win with Microsoft Security
Empower your customers with the value of Microsoft Security. Join us for a reoccurring digital training series designed to help CSP partners confidently position Microsoft Security solutions. Each session alternates between two key focus areas—Data Security and Threat Protection—to build partner expertise across the full Microsoft Security portfolio. The next event is scheduled for Monday, March 18. Learn more and register: Americas, EMEA, Asia.

  • Learn about Microsoft Security licensing and Defender and Purview Suites.
  • Access go-to-market resources, demos, and skilling paths.
  • Discover current promotions and incentives to activate.
  • Get actionable guidance from Microsoft Security sales experts during a live Q&A.

ANNOUNCEMENTS

Everything you need to upskill and drive demand, in one easy-to-use kit

Simplify and speed up execution across Stage 1 of the Microsoft Customer Engagement Methodology with a Partner Success Kit—complete with skilling resources to enable your team and go‑to‑market tools to launch campaigns faster.

Windows Server and SQL Server subscription update

On April 1, 2026, Microsoft is updating the product terms for Windows Server and SQL Server subscriptions purchased under the Microsoft Customer Agreement that make those subscriptions eligible for license mobility rights and ensure that subscriptions purchased through CSP have the same benefits as those provided through Software Assurance in our legacy Volume Licensing offers. Review the updated product terms.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

Copyright 2026 Microsoft Corporation.

 

You have received this message from Microsoft as a result of your participation in the Microsoft AI Cloud Partner Program. This message contains important information on your general membership, or your benefits as a member.

 
Privacy Statement
 

Microsoft Corporation
One Microsoft Way
Redmond, WA 98052

data.subject: Accepted: Call with Centrilogic
Re: information request - DB

body.content:
Hi Sarah,

Please find attached requested information. Please note the following:

Top 10 Customer MRR details includes the details for the top 10 customers as of Feb 2026. MRR tab includes MRR amount for each customer by LOB for period starting Sep 2024 to Feb 2026. I've also included several details in contact info tab.

Backlog & Sales Tracker data file includes data I've leveraged from the Sales Tracker (Sep 2021 to Feb 2026) and MRR files to gather details regarding backlog and remaining contracted revenue deals as % of MRR. 

        Backlog:
    • Backlog includes amounts for MRC ($46M). This was gathered based on remaining term for each contract that extends beyond Feb 2026.
    • Backlog for NRC (excluding PS) is ($141K). This was based on completions of NRC deals.
    • For the PS backlog I've used the data from PS revenue sheet ($431K). This would be the remaining amounts unrecognized from the sheet.

       % customer revenue on contract vs MRR:
    • While leveraging the sales results file and looking up contracted amounts that have remaining term, this shows these orders consist of $3.1M of our $5.8M MRR for Feb 2026. This would constitute 54% of our MRR. If 54% of our MRC is still under contract, would that mean that remaining 46% of MRR would have services which are on a Month to Month term?
    • The total % of MRR for these customers would constitute 67% of our MRR, but some of the services for these customers may have come out of contract.
    • The average contract term for these deals (excludes HWM, or one time items), would be 28 months, while the average term remaining on these deals would be 14 months.

Please let me know if this works for request, or if any questions.

Thanks,

Paul Billard
Financial Analyst

aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>
Sent: March 17, 2026 9:31 AM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: information request - DB
 
Hi Kathy/Tom, 

I'm not sure if you are the correct person to ask this, but do either of you maintain the renewals tracker? If so, can you please send the most up-to-date version of this file? This would be required to get a sense of how much of our revenues is contracted vs on a month to month basis.

@Jorge Quintero do we have a report that can provide remaining term per Service ID#?

Thanks,

Paul Billard
Financial Analyst

aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>
Sent: March 16, 2026 4:55 PM
To: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: information request - DB
 
HI Sarah,

For contribution margin,  I only have the high level general margins by LOB.  

If DB wants it per customer's MRR,   I think utilizing the profitability data Jorge put together will be best.  And contribution margin would be Revenues less any direct costs associated with customer,  excluding rent and other overheads that isnt captured by Jorge.   


Dennis Lam
Manager, Finance

+1 (604) 909-6386
aptum.com | LinkedIn



From: Sarah Blanchard <Sarah.Blanchard@aptum.com>
Sent: Monday, March 16, 2026 1:44 PM
To: Dennis Lam <Dennis.Lam@aptum.com>; Paul Billard <Paul.Billard@aptum.com>; Jorge Quintero <Jorge.Quintero@aptum.com>; Adam Nelson <Adam.Nelson@aptum.com>; Kathy Wu <Kathy.Wu@aptum.com>; Tom Kelsch <Tom.Kelsch@aptum.com>; George Revie <George.Revie@aptum.com>
Subject: information request - DB
 

Hey Team,

 

We do have a request coming in for updated information for Digital Bridge. For each of the below please indicate timing to which you can provide the requested information if you can’t provide it by EOD tomorrow. I tagged who can provide the info to the best of my knowledge, but feel free to collaborate on these.

 

  • Details on top 10 clients, including MRR (@Paul Billard), scope (what LOB @Paul Billard), contract expiry (@Adam Nelson and @Kathy Wu or @Tom Kelsch) and margin  (For margin let’s use the contribution margin here please and add a note to what is the contribution margin (@Paul Billard and @Dennis Lam)
    1. Also include customer demographics (e.g. Industry and region) @Paul Billard and @Tom Kelsch please collaborate on this
  • % of revenue on contract vs month-to-month and average remaining contract term @Paul Billard you can leverage the Run Rate file and the renewal tracker.

What should be our recommended approach here?

It seems we either need to: 
  1. Tell the customer it can't continue, in which case they will churn (they have expressed the stance to keep the same licence - "it's always been that way")
  2. Stay as is and hope for the best.

I don't think that the second option is an option.

Senior Director, Account Management

Cc: Information Security <information_security@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.

I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has installed or is using software and violating the license agreement, should Aptum be policing that at all? If we misinterpret a license agreement, couldn't that make Aptum liable? (I'm not saying Charles has misinterpreted it in this ticket, more as a general thought).

This ticket likely slipped through the cracks during the transition periods mentioned above, and also because, like IT, the InfoSec team don't typically look at the "Aptum" project in Jira - we have dedicated Jira projects for Security work.

Cheers,
Fred

data.subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification

body.content:
All,

I want to close the loop on this. This ticket has been open for nearly a year, it's been passed around between multiple people (several of whom are no longer here). 

A few things I want to clarify up front:

  1. This is not an Abuse issue. Abuse handles misuse of our services illegal activity like bitcoin mining. This is a commercial licensing gap. Fred TY, your team can stand down on this one. That said, we do need to clearly define what routes to Abuse vs. what's a commercial matter so we don't repeat this.
  2. Someone needs to own this. @Elizabeth Perez since you're already in communication with the customer, can you take the lead? Frederic and Barbara, please support as needed from the commercial and SDM side.
  3. The economics are what they are. Smart Scale pays us roughly $2,500/month. The compliant licensing would cost them ~$7,900/month on top of that. They're not going to pay that. Threatening suspension hasn't worked for a year and it's not going to start working now.

We are currently liable and at risk as an organization if we do nothing!

Here's what I think we should do:

@Elizabeth Perez, schedule a call with Mario (let's stop the back and forth email chains). He clearly doesn't understand (or doesn't want to understand) the issue, and a year of back-and-forth hasn't helped. I feel like we should present the following options:
  • They can purchase per-core licenses through us (the expensive path, don't lead with it, but it is an option)
  • They can procure Software Assurance directly from Microsoft and give us the License Mobility form (somebody to confirm... @Andy Petterson?)
  • We explore whether migrating their SQL workloads to Azure SQL makes sense, licensing is bundled in, the mobility problem goes away, and we potentially keep the relationship
    • Frederic flagged the Azure idea and I think it's worth exploring. @Andy Petterson, is it possible to get a cost comparison between the two?
  • If none of the above work, we agree on a timeline for them to move off our platform in an orderly way

Whatever comes out of the call, put the agreed path and deadline in writing. If they don't act, we follow through. No more open-ended limbo.

Let's get this resolved in the next two weeks. If anyone has questions or sees a gap in this, let me know.

Adam Nelson
VP, Operations

+1 (438) 802-9796
aptum.com | LinkedIn

Cc: Information Security <information_security@aptum.com>
Subject: Re: Smart - Scale - Ticket 12028 - Smart Scale - SQL Server License Verification
 
When the previous manager of Abuse (Joseph) was exited last year, a lot of historical knowledge was lost. It's also worth clarifying that he was a 1-man team; he had no reports. His Abuse responsibilities were given to InfoSec to handle as best we can, and since then, we have been trying to document some of that knowledge based on our interpretation of what Abuse should handle, which does align with what Adam said: the mis-use of our services.

I don't believe license (mis-)use should fall under the purview of Abuse. If a customer has installed or is using software and violating the license agreement, should Aptum be policing that at all? If we misinterpret a license agreement, couldn't that make Aptum liable? (I'm not saying Charles has misinterpreted it in this ticket, more as a general thought).

This ticket likely slipped through the cracks during the transition periods mentioned above, and also because, like IT, the InfoSec team don't typically look at the "Aptum" project in Jira - we have dedicated Jira projects for Security work.

Fred

  • Details on top 10 clients, including MRR (@Paul Billard), scope (what LOB @Paul Billard), contract expiry (@Adam Nelson and @Kathy Wu or @Tom Kelsch) and margin  (For margin let’s use the contribution margin here please and add a note to what is the contribution margin (@Paul Billard and @Dennis Lam)

Subject: RE: AWS Cloud Operations Competency App-0059154 - Aptum

 

You don't often get email from pacolor@amazon.es. Learn why this is important

Hello team,

 

Thank you again for your interest in the AWS Competency Program. Our Segment team has completed their review and have identified a few areas where providing additional information or clarification could strengthen your case studies and ensure they better align with the program's requirements. Please take a moment to review the recommendations below.

 

Action Items:

Please address the following items for remediation flagged by our Segment team to continue processing your application.  To ensure your application is processed, please provide updates by date – within 10 business days. Applications for AWS Cloud Operations Competency will remain open for 10 business days at which point your application will be closed until we hear back from you.

 

Thank you for submitting your self-assessment for the AWS Cloud Operations Competency — Monitoring & Observability category. I have completed our Business Review and are pleased to share that Aptum demonstrates strong technical alignment with the competency requirements. Your long-standing investment in Datadog, Elastic SIEM, and a mature managed cloud practice provides a compelling foundation.

 

That said, we have identified few documentation gaps that must be addressed before your application can advance to Technical Validation. Please review the feedback below and take the recommended actions prior to resubmission.

 

Overall Recommendation: Apply — After Gap Remediation

 

Estimated Time to Readiness: 2–4 weeks Confidence Level: High — gaps are documentation-related, not capability-related

Section 1: Required Documentation Uplifts

� Dashboard Access Control Policies (COAMO-002)

 

What is missing: Your self-assessment does not include documentation of how customer dashboard access is controlled within Datadog. The checklist requires explicit evidence of role-based access control (RBAC) policies governing who can view, edit, and manage observability dashboards.

 

What is required:

 

    1. A written description of your Datadog RBAC model (roles, permissions, and access tiers)

    2. At least one customer-specific example showing how access was configured (e.g., Futurion or Pool Supplies Canada)

    3. Supporting evidence such as a screenshot, configuration export, or Confluence runbook excerpt

 

Why this matters: This is a mandatory checklist item. Without it, the application will be flagged as incomplete during Technical Validation.

� Reference Architecture Diagrams (COAMO-001, COAMO-003, COAMO-005)

 

What is missing: Three reference architecture diagrams are required as formal evidence submissions and have not been confirmed as audit-ready:

 

    1. Monitoring & Observability Baseline (COAMO-001) — end-to-end telemetry architecture

    2. Container Observability (COAMO-003) — ECS/Fargate + Datadog APM architecture

    3. Synthetic Monitoring (COAMO-005) — Datadog synthetic test architecture with CloudWatch integration

 

What is required:

 

    1. Each diagram must show AWS services used, third-party tooling, data flows, and integration points

    2. Diagrams must be in a shareable format (PDF, PNG, or draw.io) and uploaded to your Partner Central application folder

    3. Diagrams should reference the specific customer deployment they represent (e.g., Pool Supplies Canada for container observability)

 

Why this matters: Architecture diagrams are a mandatory deliverable for each selected use case. Reviewers will use these to validate technical depth and AWS service integration.

 

� Quantifiable KPIs with Baseline Metrics (EXBL-003)

 

What is missing: Several case studies reference KPIs and outcomes but do not include before/after baseline comparisons. The checklist requires measurable improvements with quantified starting points.

 

What is required: For each of the 4 case studies, provide at least 2 KPIs in the following format:

 

   1. Metric name (e.g., Mean Time to Detect, Alert Noise Ratio, Uptime %)

    2. Baseline value (before Aptum engagement)

    3. Achieved value (after implementation)

    4. Timeframe for the improvement

 

Section 2: Items Correctly Addressed — No Action Required

 

The following areas were reviewed and found to be fully compliant:

 

    ✅ COAMO-001 Baseline Telemetry — Comprehensive coverage of infrastructure, service, and application layers

    ✅ COAMO-003 Container Observability — Two production examples (Pool Supplies ECS Fargate, Vimana Kubernetes) exceed the minimum requirement

    ✅ COAMO-005 Synthetic Monitoring — Clear evidence across Futurion and Heilind Electronics

    ✅ COAMO-006 Hybrid Cloud — Correctly marked N/A; no action required

    ✅ COAMO-007 GenAI Monitoring — Correctly excluded; aspirational responses noted but not penalized

    ✅ COBL-001 through COBL-004 — Baseline requirements met via Terraform + GitLab CI/CD across all case studies

 

Status: Conditonally Approved BR - Please update the response with the above recommendation before Technical Validation. Thank You.

 

If you have not yet explored the AWS Partner Specialization Program Benefits Guide, please make sure to see what will be waiting for you when you achieve your designation.

 

Thank you,

 

 

 

Francisco Lorenzo Fernández-Shaw

Tech Business Developer – PPS Team

Amazon Web Services – Spain | Madrid

pacolor@amazon.es
